2604.16286.title: ASMR-Bench: Auditing for Sabotage in ML Research
2604.16286.paper_summary: As AI systems are increasingly used to conduct research autonomously, misaligned systems could introduce subtle flaws that produce misleading results while evading detection. We introduce ASMR-Bench (Auditing for Sabotage in ML Research), a benchmark for evaluating the ability of auditors to detect sabotage in ML research codebases. ASMR-Bench consists of 9 ML research codebases with sabotaged variants that produce qualitatively different experimental results. Each sabotage modifies implementation details, such as hyperparameters, training data, or evaluation code, while preserving the high-level methodology described in the paper. We evaluated frontier LLMs and LLM-assisted human auditors on ASMR-Bench and found that both struggled to reliably detect sabotage: the best performance was an AUROC of 0.77 and a top-1 fix rate of 42%, achieved by Gemini 3.1 Pro. We also tested LLMs as red teamers and found that LLM-generated sabotages were weaker than human-generated ones but still sometimes evaded same-capability LLM auditors. We release ASMR-Bench to support research on monitoring and auditing techniques for AI-conducted research.
2604.16286.paper_summary_zh: 隨著 AI 系統越來越多地被用來自主進行研究，未對齊的系統可能會引入微妙的缺陷，從而產生誤導性的結果而不被檢測到。我們介紹 ASMR-Bench（機器學習研究中的破壞審計），這是一個用於評估審計員檢測機器學習研究代碼庫中破壞能力的基準。ASMR-Bench 包含 9 個機器學習研究代碼庫，並具有產生質量上不同實驗結果的破壞變體。每個破壞都修改了實現細節，例如超參數、訓練數據或評估代碼，同時保留了論文中描述的高層次方法論。我們在 ASMR-Bench 上評估了前沿的 LLM 和 LLM 輔助的人類審計員，發現兩者都難以可靠地檢測破壞：最佳表現是 AUROC 0.77 和 42% 的前 1 修復率，由 Gemini 3.1 Pro 實現。我們還測試了 LLM 作為紅隊成員，發現 LLM 生成的破壞比人類生成的弱，但有時仍能逃避同等能力的 LLM 審計員。我們發布 ASMR-Bench 以支持對 AI 進行研究的監控和審計技術的研究。
2604.16286.author: Eric Gan et.al.
2604.16286.authors: Eric Gan, Aryan Bhatt, Buck Shlegeris, Julian Stastny, Vivek Hebbar
